_meta.description: Pre-built context blocks for each agent in the pipeline. Each agent gets only the context it needs — not the entire knowledge base.
_meta.usage: Agent code loads this file and injects the relevant context_block into its system prompt

agent_1_scenario_planner.purpose: Gives the Scenario Planner enough context to create diverse, realistic student profiles
agent_1_scenario_planner.context_block:
PHYSICSWALLAH CONTEXT FOR SCENARIO GENERATION:

Available Courses:
- JEE: Lakshya (Main+Advanced, ₹35,999), Arjuna (Main only, ₹19,999), Prayas (Foundation Class 9-10, ₹14,999)
- NEET: Yakeen (₹33,999), Sankalp Dropper Batch (₹44,999)
- Boards: Udaan (₹5,999)

Student Demographics to Vary:
- Cities: Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore, Hyderabad, Kota, Patna, Jaipur, Lucknow, Pune, Kolkata, Ranchi, Bhopal, Guwahati, Chandigarh, Indore, Varanasi, Chennai, Coimbatore, Surat, Ahmedabad
- Classes: 9, 10, 11, 12, Dropper
- Exams: JEE Main, JEE Main+Advanced, NEET, Board Exams
- Budget attitudes: Comfortable (30%), Moderate (40%), Budget-conscious (20%), Price not a factor (10%)
- Common competitors they might mention: Allen, FIITJEE, Unacademy, BYJU'S, Aakash

Objection Types to Include:
- Price concern, Need parent approval, Already in other coaching, Just exploring, Too early/late to start, Not sure about coaching value

Personality Variations:
- Enthusiastic and eager, Shy and brief responses, Analytical and question-heavy, Skeptical and resistant, Confused and undecided

IMPORTANT: No two scenarios should have the same (name + city + exam + class + personality) combination.

agent_2_conversation_generator.purpose: Gives the Conversation Generator detailed course info and agent persona for realistic dialogue
agent_2_conversation_generator.context_block:
PHYSICSWALLAH SALES CONTEXT:

Agent Persona: Priya, AI Sales Assistant at PhysicsWallah
- Warm, friendly, professional
- Patient — never rushes
- Uses mix of English and Hindi naturally
- Empathetic to budget concerns
- Encouraging but never pushy

Course Details (Use these EXACT names and prices in conversation):

JEE COURSES:
1. Lakshya (JEE Main + Advanced) — ₹35,999/year (EMI: ₹3,499/month)
   - 6 live classes/week, daily practice, weekly tests, 24/7 doubt solving
   - Faculty: Alakh Pandey (Physics), Pankaj Sijairya (Chemistry), Amit Mahajan (Maths)
   - Next batch: March 15, 2026
   - Timings: Morning (6-8 AM), Evening (5-7 PM), Weekend (9 AM-1 PM)

2. Arjuna (JEE Main only) — ₹19,999/year (EMI: ₹1,999/month)
   - 4 live classes/week, bi-weekly tests, doubt sessions
   - Next batch: March 1, 2026

3. Prayas (Foundation, Class 9-10) — ₹14,999/year (EMI: ₹1,499/month)
   - 3 classes/week, school board + competitive coverage
   - Next batch: April 1, 2026

NEET COURSES:
4. Yakeen (NEET UG) — ₹33,999/year (EMI: ₹3,299/month)
   - 6 live classes/week, NCERT line-by-line, specimen videos
   - Faculty: Alakh Pandey, Dr. Anand Mani (Biology)
   - Next batch: March 15, 2026

5. Sankalp (NEET Dropper) — ₹44,999/10 months (EMI: ₹4,499/month)
   - 8 classes/week intensive, one-on-one mentoring
   - Next batch: March 1, 2026

BOARD COURSES:
6. Udaan (Class 12 Boards) — ₹5,999 (no EMI needed)
   - Self-paced recorded + live doubt sessions
   - Available now

FINANCIAL OPTIONS:
- PW Pay: 0% EMI on 3-month plans (₹199 processing fee)
- Bank EMI: HDFC/ICICI/SBI, up to 24 months, 8.5% p.a.
- Scholarship: Monthly PWST exam → 10% to 90% waiver
- Merit: 25-50% off for 90%+ board scores
- Need-based: Up to 100% for income < ₹3 lakh/year
- Refund: Full refund within 7 days

OBJECTION RESPONSES:
- 'Too expensive' → EMI options, scholarship test, comparison with Allen/FIITJEE (3-5x more expensive)
- 'Need parents' → Offer to schedule family call or send WhatsApp brochure
- 'Already in coaching' → PW can supplement, or highlight switching benefits
- 'Just exploring' → Respect that, offer to send info, schedule follow-up
- 'Not sure about coaching' → Acknowledge self-study, highlight specific value-adds

CONVERSATION RULES:
- Always ask Tier 1 questions: Name, Class, Exam, Timeline, City, Decision-maker
- Then Tier 2: Current coaching, Start urgency, Budget, Mode preference, Lead source
- Maximum 10 minutes / 15-20 exchanges
- End with clear next steps
- Never be pushy

agent_3_extractor_scorer.purpose: Gives the Extractor context on valid field values for validation
agent_3_extractor_scorer.context_block:
VALID EXTRACTION VALUES:

Courses mentioned in conversation should map to:
- Lakshya, Arjuna, Prayas (JEE courses)
- Yakeen, Sankalp (NEET courses)
- Udaan (Board course)

Valid class values: 9, 10, 11, 12, dropper
Valid exam values: JEE Main, JEE Advanced, JEE Main+Advanced, NEET, Board Exams
Valid urgency levels: high (exam < 12 months), medium (12-18 months), low (18+ months)
Valid engagement levels: high (asks questions, detailed responses, enthusiastic), medium (answers questions but brief), low (one-word answers, wants to end call)

Scoring context:
- Budget 'comfortable' → student mentions ₹30K-50K range or says 'not an issue'
- Budget 'moderate' → interested but asks about EMI/discounts
- Budget 'concerned' → says 'too expensive', 'need to check', 'can't afford'
- Parent support: true if parent is present or student says parents are supportive
- Competitor flag: set to competitor name if student mentions Allen/FIITJEE/Unacademy/BYJU'S/Aakash

agent_4_qa_validator.purpose: Gives QA agent criteria for quality checks
agent_4_qa_validator.context_block:
QA VALIDATION CRITERIA:

1. CONVERSATION REALISM CHECK:
- Does it feel like a real phone conversation? (not robotic or scripted)
- Does the student have a consistent personality throughout?
- Are the agent responses natural and contextually appropriate?
- Is there appropriate back-and-forth (not just Q&A)?
- Are objections handled conversationally (not with canned responses)?

2. DATA COMPLETENESS CHECK:
- Required fields (must be non-null): full_name, current_class, target_exam
- Expected fields (should be non-null for 80%+ of conversations): exam_year, location, engagement_level
- If a required field is null, flag for re-generation

3. SCORE-CONVERSATION ALIGNMENT:
- HOT lead conversations should show: urgent timeline, budget OK, high engagement, clear intent
- WARM lead conversations should show: interest but hesitation, need parent approval, or timeline not urgent
- COLD lead conversations should show: just exploring, distant timeline, low engagement, or significant objections
- Flag if score doesn't match conversation tone

4. DISTRIBUTION CHECK:
- Target: 33 HOT, 33 WARM, 34 COLD (±3 tolerance)
- If distribution drifts, flag which tier is over/under-represented

5. DUPLICATE DETECTION:
- Flag if two conversations have same (name + city + exam) combination
- Flag if opening 3 messages are identical across any two conversations

6. PW ACCURACY CHECK:
- Course names should be from: Lakshya, Arjuna, Prayas, Yakeen, Sankalp, Udaan
- Prices should be within 10% of knowledge base values
- Flag any course name or price that doesn't match
